title: Structural producer-consumer relationships between independent projects

decision.summary: LAB, MammothSkills, and Symphonie remain independent projects, but may establish explicit structural relationships when a workflow requires requirements exchange, skill production or adaptation, consumer feedback, validation, and direct consumption of approved artifacts.
rules: Project independence does not prohibit structural relationships.
Before creating or adapting a skill, MammothSkills must determine intended use, consumer, phase, runtime agent, target platform, input contract, output contract, constraints, and validation criteria.
A consumer such as Symphonie may provide requirements, examples, compatibility constraints, handoff schemas, and integration feedback to MammothSkills.
MammothSkills may create new skills or adapt existing skills for Claude or Codex according to the declared target platform and consumer contract.
Symphonie may consume an approved skill directly from MammothSkills as the canonical source, while the skill itself runs in the declared runtime, Claude or Codex.
Direct consumption means using an identified MammothSkills artifact or release, not depending on an untracked copy or allowing MammothSkills to write autonomously into the consumer repository.
Consumer fit and runtime compatibility are separate validations from technical adaptation.
Consumer feedback may trigger a new MammothSkills revision, but does not itself authorize implementation, release, installation, or integration.
Every structural relationship must identify consumer, consumer phase, runtime, target platform, inputs, outputs, validation evidence, and consumed version or artifact identifier.

project_roles.LAB: Evaluate viability, feasibility, risks, integrations, and project initiation; preserve validated cross-project knowledge.
project_roles.MammothSkills: Create, adapt, audit, test, repair, version, and publish skills for declared agent platforms and consumer contracts.
project_roles.Symphonie: Operate an eight-phase workflow for creating applications and websites using Claude and Codex, and specify, validate, and consume skills when required by its phases.

relationship_model.runtime_location: Claude or Codex according to target_platform
